what_it_does: Defines a static HTML design piece showing two phone mockups for comparing an earlier take and the current take, with stacked visual cards, a connector, preference controls, and a plain-language change list.
observations: Loads Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono, plus shared piece and phone stylesheets.
Uses inline CSS to define take cards, take labels, a vertical joint connector, and rounded verdict controls.
The large phone stacks an "earlier take" above "this take" and separates them with a socket, dashed line, and plug motif.
The current take uses three colored panels while the earlier take uses two page-colored panels.
The second phone contains a "WHAT CHANGED" view populated from a local JavaScript array.
The active verdict is "Prefer this" and the primary action reads "Keep This Take".
standouts: Comparison is presented as two stacked takes rather than a side-by-side diff.
A physical joint metaphor visually carries continuity between the two takes.
The file pairs visual comparison with a concise generated list of textual changes.
conventions: Follows the surrounding piece pattern with a `.view`, title block, `.phones`, `.unit`, phone shells, role captions, mark, and foot elements.
Uses design tokens such as `--dna-space-*`, `--line`, `--gold`, `--warm`, `--cool`, and `--nested` for spacing, color, and radius.
Uses inline HTML styles for small per-panel background choices.
Uses a small script-side data array to render repeated change rows instead of hardcoding every row in markup.
concerns: The change-row data includes unused empty string values in each tuple.
Several dimensions and offsets are fixed in pixels, which may make this composition tightly bound to the current phone mockup size.
Inline styles on `.pl` elements mix instance styling into markup.
notes: Brief someone that this file is a static compare-screen mockup where stacked takes, a connector, and a change list explain what changed.
questions: What is the intended meaning of the unused second value in each `C` array entry?
Is the connector meant to represent navigation, comparison continuity, or a physical handoff between takes?
purpose_vs_actual: The title and on-page copy state a compare screen, and the file implements a compare-oriented phone mockup with earlier/current takes, preference controls, and a changes view.
apparent_themes: mobile UI mockup as design substrate
intention_signals: It seems to be exploring a mobile compare experience where a user can inspect two takes, see what changed in words, choose a preferred take, and keep the current one.
novelty: The compare relationship is expressed through a vertical socket-dash-plug joint between stacked takes.
contribution: Contributes a compact mobile comparison pattern that combines stacked visual states, a continuity connector, explicit preference controls, and a generated plain-language change ledger.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML/CSS/JS design piece showing a two-phone mobile compare screen with stacked earlier/current takes, a connector motif, preference pills, and generated change rows.
intention_for_embedding: Created to explore how an app screen can present what changed between design takes and let the user prefer or keep one take.
